testCases.query: I need to implement a full authentication system: user model, auth endpoints, JWT tokens, middleware, and integration tests. Help me break this down into trackable tasks with proper dependencies.
expectedBehavior: Claude decomposes the work into atomic tasks using TaskCreate
Establishes dependency chains with addBlockedBy (e.g., auth endpoints blocked by user model)
Sets proper status workflow: pending for all initially, then in_progress as work begins
Provides activeForm for each task (e.g., 'Creating User model', 'Implementing JWT tokens')
Uses TaskUpdate to track progress as tasks complete

testCases.query: We have a cross-cutting refactor that needs a backend architect and frontend dev working simultaneously on shared contracts. Should I use Task tool subagents or Agent Teams?
expectedBehavior: Claude evaluates the criteria table: cross-cutting changes, agents need to communicate
Recommends Agent Teams over Task tool for this scenario due to mesh communication needs
Explains TeamCreate, peer-to-peer SendMessage, and shared task list patterns
Warns about the higher cost (~2.5x) of Agent Teams vs Task tool subagents

testCases.query: Change the button color from blue to green in the header component
expectedBehavior: Claude does NOT invoke the task-dependency-patterns skill
Makes the simple CSS/style change directly without task decomposition
Does not create TaskCreate calls for trivial single-step work

testCases.query: I have two agents both trying to work on the same database migration task. How do I prevent conflicts?
expectedBehavior: Requires agents to claim task ownership atomically before starting work on it
Uses TaskUpdate with owner field set to the claiming agent's identifier
Prevents two agents from simultaneously working on the same task without ownership
Coordinates through the shared task list and messaging tools rather than direct state sharing

testCases.query: My downstream task started failing because the upstream task was marked complete too early. How should I handle completion?
expectedBehavior: Verifies all implementation is done and tests pass before marking a task as completed
Warns that premature completion cascades errors to dependent tasks in the chain
Requires running the test suite and confirming all green before status transition
Documents what was done and any caveats in the task completion notes

testCases.query: How do I check if a task's blockers are resolved before I start working on it?
expectedBehavior: Uses TaskGet to check the blockedBy array before transitioning to in_progress status
Validates that all blocking tasks have completed status before starting dependent work
Detects circular dependencies that would create deadlocks in the task graph
Refuses to start work on a task when any of its blockers are still pending or in progress
